name: Cartoon
description: Cartoon and animation-style short-form video: 2D, cel-shaded, hand-drawn, rubber-hose, flat-vector, watercolor, pixel, neon line, manga, claymation, doodle, or 3D toon-shading. 5-15s, 9:16, vibe reference only — copy the style language, not the literal scene. Lean on classic 12 animation principles (squash-stretch, anticipation, follow-through, arcs) and a strong 2-second hook. Use whenever a brief asks for cartoon, animation, illustrated, animated, fun, playful, whimsical, or any non-photoreal stylized look.

stackSummary.keyframes: google/gemini-3-pro-image-preview via OpenRouter — one keyframe per scene in the chosen cartoon style. Lock style declarators (line weight, palette, art direction) in every prompt so frames stay model-consistent.
stackSummary.i2v: kwaivgi/kling-v3.0-pro via OpenRouter, 5s i2v clips, generate_audio: false. Push squash-stretch, anticipation, follow-through, and arc motion in the motion brief — animation principles read better than literal action verbs.
stackSummary.voiceover: ElevenLabs eleven_multilingual_v2. Tone matches the cartoon sub-style: bouncy/comedic for Cartoon Network, gentle/wonderstruck for Disney/Ghibli, deadpan-narrator for flat-vector explainers. Language follows the brief.
stackSummary.captions: OpenRouter whisper-1 + HormoziCaptions or KaraokeCaptions in Remotion — bold and animated to match the cartoon energy. Subtle/MinimalCaptions only for watercolor or oil-painting styles.
stackSummary.music: ElevenLabs Music — match the sub-style: jazz/honk for rubber-hose, upbeat electro for flat vector, orchestral whimsy for Disney/Ghibli, chiptune for pixel art, synthwave for neon. SFX (boing, whoosh, comedic stab, slide-whistle) layered in composition.

constants.primaryStyle: pick exactly one cartoon style and stick to it across all keyframes

variationAxes: art style (Disney, Cartoon Network, flat vector, rubber hose, pencil sketch, watercolor, paper cutout, pixel art, neon line, manga/anime, claymation, oil painting, silhouette, doodle, 3D toon-shaded)
line work (thick bold 4-8px, thin uniform 1-2px, variable gestural, no-outline color-only, sketchy textured)
color palette (bold saturated, pastel soft, monochrome, warm nostalgic, cool calm, complementary high-contrast, earth tones, neon cyberpunk)
background approach (detailed painted, simplified graphic, abstract gradient, repeating pattern, fully dynamic shape-shifting)
framerate target (12fps for retro/claymation choppiness, 24fps for theatrical, 30-60fps for flat vector and neon)
tone (slapstick comedy, peaceful Ghibli, modern explainer, surreal whimsy, dramatic anime)
doNotCopyLiterally: Pick ONE primary style per project. Do not blend Disney smooth curves with Cartoon Network bold outlines in the same scene — conflicting style stacks generate muddy frames. Vibe reference only: never lift exact characters, exact palette hexes, or named-IP designs (no Mickey, no Pikachu, no Bart).
referenceNotes: No user reference required by default — cartoon is a stylized look, not a real-entity reproduction. EXCEPTION: if the brief names a real brand mascot, real person, or specific franchise character, the AGENTS.md hard rule #3 reference gate applies and a user-supplied image is required.
